title: Fix stutters
body:
Podman adds an Error: to every error message. So starting an error
message with "error" ends up being reported to the user as

Error: error ...

This patch removes the stutter.

Also ioutil.ReadFile errors report the Path, so wrapping the err message
with the path causes a stutter.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh dwalsh@redhat.com

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Does this mean we stop mentioning that errors are errors when people run the `buildah` command?
All kinds of unhappy tests here @rhatdan

@nalind I think most errors are clearly errors when they happen, but we can do the same as Podman and prepend Errors: if you think we should.

@flouthoc @TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind @vrothberg @giuseppe PTAL

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc*, *giuseppe*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,giuseppe,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @nalind I think most errors are clearly errors when they happen, but we can do the same as Podman and prepend Errors: if you think we should.

The stutter you're trying to fix here only exists because podman does that, so yeah, I'd say we should.  To put a fine point on it, if fixing podman's stutter means making buildah's error messages less clear, I'm going to nack.
